title: There Was A Man
content:
There Was A Man.


                    There was a man, and he had nought,
                        And robbers came to rob him;



                    He crept up to the chimney-pot,





                    And then they could not find him;



                    He ran fourteen miles in fifteen days,
                        And never looked behind him.
meaning:
Leonard Leslie Brooke's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "There Was A Man"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
